title: AN ACT CREATING A BARANGAY TO BE KNOWN AS BARANGAY TIMBABAUAN IN THE MUNICIPALITY OF TUNGAWAN, PROVINCE OF ZAMBOANGA SIBUGAY

chunks.text:
115 OG No. 22, 5431 (June 3, 2019)

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 11275, April 12, 2019 ]

AN ACT CREATING A BARANGAY TO BE KNOWN AS BARANGAY TIMBABAUAN IN THE MUNICIPALITY OF TUNGAWAN, PROVINCE OF ZAMBOANGA SIBUGAY

Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the Philippines in Congress assembled:

SECTION 1.

Creation of Barangay Timbabauan. -

There is hereby created a barangay to be known as Barangay Timbabauan in the Municipality of Tungawan, Province of Zamboanga Sibugay.

SEC. 3.

Conduct and Supervision of Plebiscite.

- The plebiscite conducted and supervised by the Commission on Elections (COMELEC) pursuant to Provincial Ordinance No. 11, series of 1991, dated October 8, 1991 of the Sangguniang Panlalawigan of the Province of Zamboanga del Sur, which ratified the creation of Barangay Timbabauan as proclaimed by the Plebiscite Board of Canvassers on December 2, 1994 shall serve as substantial compliance with the plebiscite requirement under Section 10 of Republic Act No. 7160, as amended, otherwise known as the "Local Government Code of 1991".

SEC. 4.

Incumbent Barangay Officials. -

The incumbent barangay officials of Barangay Timbabauan shall continue to hold office until such time that their successors have been duly elected and qualified.

SEC. 5.

Public Infrastructure and Facilities.

- All existing public infrastructure and facilities for public use therein at the time of the approval of this Act are hereby transferred without cost or compensation to and shall be administered by the new barangay.

SEC. 6.

Internal Revenue Allotment Shares. -

Barangay Timbabauan shall be entitled to Internal Revenue Allotment shares pursuant to Section 285 of Republic Act No. 7160, as amended, otherwise known as the "Local Government Code of 1991".

SEC. 7.

Effectivity. -

This Act shall take effect fifteen (15) days after its publication in the

Official Gazette

or in a newspaper of general circulation.

Approved,

(SGD.)

VICENTE C. SOTTO III

President of the Senate

(SGD.)

GLORIA MACAPAGAL-ARROYO

Speaker of the House of Representatives

This Act which originated in the House of Representatives was passed by the House of Representatives on October 9, 2017, amended by the Senate of the Philippines on February 6, 2019, and which amendments were concurred in by the House of Representatives on February 7, 2019.

(SGD.)

MYRA MARIE D. VILLARICA

Secretary of the Senate

(SGD.)

DANTE ROBERTO P. MALING

Acting Secretary General House of Representatives

Approved: APR 12 2019

(SGD.)

RODRIGO ROA DUTERTE

President of the Philippines
